Subject: City of Aurora - Noise Equity and EJ Analysis Deficiency
nepa_comment_body__c: The Draft EIS noise analysis relies on a 65 dB DNL single-metric threshold that fails to capture low-altitude overflight impacts on communities below that threshold. The City of Aurora contains 14 Title I school facilities that fall between 55-64 dB DNL under Alternative B. The environmental justice analysis does not assess disproportionate impacts on Aurora's 45% Hispanic/Latino population in affected census tracts as required by CEQ's 2023 Updated EJ Guidance and DOT Order 5610.2C. The FEIS must include a frequency-of-overflight analysis and a complete demographic overlay of all noise-impacted census tracts.
nepa_category__c: Environmental Justice - Noise Equity; EJ Analysis
nepa_agency_response__c: FEIS updated with frequency-of-overflight analysis for all communities between 55-65 dB DNL. Environmental justice analysis expanded per CEQ 2023 EJ Guidance with demographic overlays for all census tracts receiving 10 or more daily operations under Alternative B. Mitigation commitments include community noise ombudsman program and voluntary soundproofing assistance for homes within 65 dB DNL contour.
nepa_organization__c: City of Aurora - Community Development Department

Subject: Colorado Coalition for Clean Air - PM2.5 Cumulative Impact Analysis
nepa_comment_body__c: The Draft EIS air quality analysis uses a 2019 baseline year that predates both the COVID-19 operational reduction period and the post-pandemic traffic recovery surge. The cumulative impact analysis does not account for the DEN South Terminal expansion (2022) or the planned Concourse O addition (2026). Under NAAQS standards for PM2.5, the 2.5-mile school proximity zone analysis must be updated to reflect current operations and projected 2030 fleet mix. The EPA 2022 PM2.5 NAAQS tightening to 9 ug/m3 must be applied.
nepa_agency_response__c: Air quality analysis updated with 2022 baseline and 2030 project fleet mix. Cumulative impact analysis incorporates DEN South Terminal expansion. PM2.5 analysis updated to reflect EPA's revised 9 ug/m3 NAAQS standard. School proximity modeling updated for all facilities within 2.5 miles of significant flight track changes.

Subject: Adams County Board of Commissioners - Noise Monitoring Commitments
nepa_comment_body__c: Adams County supports the preferred alternative but requests binding commitments on three points: (1) establishment of a permanent noise monitoring network with real-time public access portal, (2) annual reporting to affected local governments on actual vs. modeled noise performance, and (3) a 5-year post-implementation review with authority to implement additional mitigation if modeled noise impacts are exceeded. These commitments should appear in the ROD as enforceable conditions rather than aspirational language.
nepa_agency_response__c: ROD includes three binding mitigation commitments: (1) 12-station permanent noise monitoring network with real-time public dashboard deployed within 18 months of implementation, (2) annual noise performance reports submitted to Adams County and all affected municipalities, (3) 5-year post-implementation review with trigger thresholds for additional mitigation. Commitments are enforceable conditions of the ROD.
